description: Round 4 hardening: ReDoS prevention, sender origin validation, race condition fixes, unbounded export limits, email error handling, board extraction consolidation, merge warning, and analytics error boundary.

userStories.title: Escape regex in contacts company filter to prevent ReDoS
userStories.description: backend/contacts/service.py line 65: the company filter uses {'$regex': company, '$options': 'i'} with raw user input. Special regex characters (e.g., '(a+)+$') can cause catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS). Import re and apply re.escape(company) before passing to the $regex operator. This ensures only literal string matching occurs.
acceptanceCriteria: Company filter uses re.escape() on user input before $regex
Search still works for normal company names with special chars like 'C++' or 'Yahoo!'
A test exists proving regex metacharacters are safely escaped
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Security — HIGH severity. ReDoS can cause denial of service via crafted company search.

userStories.title: Validate sender origin in extension background message handler
userStories.description: extension/background/background.js line 189: chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener ignores the sender parameter (named _sender). Any webpage could potentially send messages to the service worker. Add validation that sender.id matches chrome.runtime.id (own extension) before processing any message. Return { error: 'Unauthorized' } for invalid senders.
acceptanceCriteria: onMessage handler validates sender.id === chrome.runtime.id
Messages from non-extension origins are rejected with error response
All extension tests pass: npm test
Normal content script → background communication still works
userStories.notes: Security — HIGH severity. Without sender validation, malicious pages could invoke API calls.

userStories.title: Fix duplicate application race condition with unique index
userStories.description: backend/applications/service.py lines 155-167: the duplicate check (find_one) and insert (insert_one) are separate non-atomic operations. Two concurrent requests can both pass the check and create duplicates. Add a unique compound index on (user_id, url_hash) to the applications collection in the database setup/migration, and catch DuplicateKeyError in the insert path to raise DuplicateApplicationError instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Unique compound index exists on applications collection for (user_id, url_hash)
insert_one catches pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError and raises DuplicateApplicationError
Concurrent identical saves do not create duplicate records
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Data integrity — MEDIUM severity. Users report occasional duplicate applications appearing.

userStories.title: Add memory limit to CSV export query
userStories.description: backend/applications/service_export.py line 33: .to_list(length=None) loads ALL applications into memory for CSV export. A user with 100k+ applications could exhaust server memory. Add MAX_EXPORT_ROWS = 50000 constant and use .to_list(length=MAX_EXPORT_ROWS). Log a structlog warning if result count equals the limit, indicating truncation. Include a comment in the CSV header row count if truncated.
acceptanceCriteria: .to_list() uses MAX_EXPORT_ROWS limit instead of None
Warning logged if export is truncated at limit
Export still works correctly for normal users (<50k applications)
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Performance — MEDIUM severity. Unbounded memory allocation is a production risk.

userStories.title: Add try-except and logging to email service SMTP calls
userStories.description: backend/notifications/email_service.py lines 83, 94, 108: three run_in_executor calls to _send_smtp have no error handling. If SMTP connection fails (timeout, auth error, network), the exception is silently lost. Wrap each run_in_executor call in try-except, catch smtplib.SMTPException and OSError, and log the failure with structlog including the to_email and error details. Do NOT re-raise — email failures should not crash the request.
acceptanceCriteria: All three run_in_executor calls wrapped in try-except
Catches smtplib.SMTPException and OSError specifically
Failures logged with structlog including recipient and error message
Email send failures do not propagate to caller
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Reliability — MEDIUM severity. Silent email failures make debugging impossible.

userStories.title: Create board extraction factory to reduce duplication across 13 board files
userStories.description: extension/content/boards/ has 13 board files (linkedin.js, indeed.js, greenhouse.js, etc.) all sharing identical structure: isJobPage(), getTitle(), getCompany(), getLocation(), extractFields(). Create a createBoardExtractor(config) factory in board_utils.js that accepts { id, urlPattern, selectors: { title: [...], company: [...], location: [...], compensation: [...] } } and returns { BOARD_ID, isJobPage, extractFields }. Refactor the 3 simplest boards (remoteok.js, dice.js, wellfound.js) to use it as proof of concept. Leave complex boards (linkedin, greenhouse) unchanged for now.
acceptanceCriteria: board_utils.js exports createBoardExtractor factory function
At least 3 board files refactored to use the factory
Refactored boards still extract fields correctly (manual verification)
All extension tests pass: npm test
userStories.notes: Code quality — MEDIUM severity. 13 files with identical structure is a maintenance burden.

userStories.title: Add explicit data loss warning to MergeDialog
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/MergeDialog.jsx: the merge dialog shows field selection UI but does NOT warn that merging permanently deletes one application record. Add a warning banner at the top of the dialog: 'Merging will combine these applications into one. The duplicate will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.' Style it with a yellow/amber warning background consistent with existing alert patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Warning banner visible at top of MergeDialog before field selection
Warning text clearly states one application will be deleted and action is irreversible
Warning uses existing alert/warning styling (amber/yellow)
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: UX safety — MEDIUM severity. Users should understand merge is destructive before confirming.

userStories.title: Wrap Analytics page charts in ErrorBoundary
userStories.description: frontend/src/pages/Analytics.jsx: AnalyticsCharts component renders Recharts visualizations with no ErrorBoundary wrapper. If chart data is malformed or Recharts throws, the entire Analytics page crashes. Wrap the charts section in an ErrorBoundary with a fallback that says 'Unable to load charts. Try refreshing the page.' and includes a retry button that calls resetErrorBoundary.
acceptanceCriteria: AnalyticsCharts wrapped in ErrorBoundary component
Fallback UI shows helpful error message with retry button
Chart crash does not take down the full Analytics page
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: UX resilience — prevents full page crash from chart rendering errors.
